data.narrative:
SkySafe, a Series B defense and security company founded by Grant Jordan, has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peer companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in Defense & Security at similar stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $95M. Peer-median analysis examines 7 same-sector, same-stage comparables including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), with a median valuation of $1.0B and a range spanning $300M to $3.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $81M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $548M. The substantial gap between methods—stage-progression anchors near the lower bound while peer-median anchors toward the upper bound—reflects material uncertainty. Key variables include SkySafe's revenue scale and growth rate relative to named peers, customer concentration, and the degree to which its technology and market position justify valuation above the peer median or toward the peer range ceiling. Additional diligence on revenue run-rate and customer acquisition trajectory would materially refine this estimate.
